config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena justru hak individu adalah batas agar kekuasaan tidak sewenang-wenang. Dalam pengalaman saya melihat kehidupan di Indonesia, alasan “demi masyarakat” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan keputusan yang merugikan orang kecil. Bagi saya, yang benar adalah mencari keseimbangan: kepentingan umum dijaga, tetapi hak dasar individu tetap dihormati.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter greatly, but they should not automatically outrank the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why many people emphasize social order, stability, and collective welfare, because a society that is chaotic or fragmented can harm everyone. But I also think that when "society" is used as a blanket excuse, individual rights can become too easy to ignore, and that creates real injustice for ordinary people. The better principle is balance: protect the common good, but keep firm limits so that basic rights are not sacrificed whenever it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Di Indonesia, saya melihat dalih “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan warga kecil, menutup kritik, atau mengorbankan orang yang posisinya lemah. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi justru hak individu harus jadi pagar supaya negara atau mayoritas tidak semena-mena. Jadi menurut saya, yang benar bukan mendahulukan masyarakat secara mutlak, melainkan menyeimbangkan kepentingan bersama dengan perlindungan hak dasar setiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically come before the individual, because that is exactly how abuse can hide behind noble language. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand the appeal of stability and collective goals, and I do think they matter in real life, especially when a society is large and complex. But I have also seen how easily “for the greater good” can become a reason to silence ordinary people, so basic rights must remain non negotiable. The right answer is not to worship individualism, but to set clear limits so the common good does not crush the person.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak keras gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membungkam kritik, merugikan warga kecil, atau membiarkan ketidakadilan atas nama stabilitas. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru hak individu harus menjadi batas yang tidak boleh dilanggar seenaknya oleh negara atau mayoritas. Jadi bagi saya, yang masuk akal adalah keseimbangan yang tegas: masyarakat dilindungi, tetapi hak dasar tiap orang tetap tidak boleh dikorbankan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the rights of the individual in a blanket way. Coming from a Chinese perspective, I understand why people value stability, order, and collective welfare, because social harmony can genuinely protect many people, but I also know how easily that logic can be abused when authorities or majorities claim to speak for “the public.” In my view, the rights of the individual must be the hard boundary, not something society can override whenever it is convenient. A healthy society serves people, and when it starts treating individual rights as disposable, it stops being just.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
